tests.name: context7_detection

tests.name: platform_module_present
tests.details: docs/platform-modules/ directory exists but is EMPTY. Framework has docs/platform-modules/mcp-server.md (hyphen) but neither init nor verify-install copies it; verify-install looks for mcp_server.md (underscore). Recorded as High framework_bug.

tests.name: no_residual_backup_dir
tests.details: .claude-backup/ absent (BUG-002 not regressed).

tests.name: pre_commit_gate_registered

tests.name: stop_checklist_registered

tests.name: pending_approval_helper_copied

tests.name: lint_uat_scenarios_helper_copied
tests.details: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh exists in project (PR #17 / U-H fix verified).

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: Running init.sh from $FRAMEWORK exits 1 with message '[FAIL] Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo.' (PR #18 fix verified).

framework_bugs.summary: Platform module not copied into project; verify-install looks for mcp_server.md but framework ships mcp-server.md
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh --non-interactive --platform mcp_server ... ; ls $PROJECT_DIR/docs/platform-modules/  (empty); bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/verify-install.sh from project; reports '[WARN] Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing (manual)'. Framework actual file is docs/platform-modules/mcp-server.md (hyphen).
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh copies the framework's platform module into docs/platform-modules/<platform>.md, OR verify-install accepts both naming conventions and matches the canonical filename used by the framework.
framework_bugs.actual: Project-side docs/platform-modules/ is empty; verify-install reports manual-action and prompts user to copy; the suggested path 'mcp_server.md' (underscore) does not exist in the framework — only 'mcp-server.md' (hyphen) does. Affects every mcp_server-platform init.

framework_bugs.summary: release.yml workflow not created by init.sh on full track
framework_bugs.expected: Per runbook Section A test 'release_workflow_created', a release.yml workflow file should exist for full-track projects.
framework_bugs.actual: .github/workflows/release.yml is not generated; only ci.yml is. Could be either init.sh missing template copy, or runbook expectation is wrong (documentation gap).

documentation_gaps.gap: verify-install.sh references docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md (underscore form derived from --platform value) but the framework's actual file is mcp-server.md (hyphen). Either rename the framework file or normalize the reference.
Runbook Section A asserts release_workflow_created, but init.sh appears to only generate ci.yml. Clarify whether release.yml is expected at init time vs. created later in Phase 4.
documentation_gaps.where: Reports/uat-2026-04-26/RUNBOOK.md Section A table; init.sh templates

tests.name: phase2_init_verify_completes
tests.details: scripts/process-checklist.sh --verify-init reports 'Phase 2 initialization fully verified (7/7 steps complete)' after stubbing Pipfile.lock and marking branch_protection_configured + data_model_applied.

tests.name: build_loop_started
tests.details: --start-feature 'uat-feat-1' set process-state.build_loop.feature.

tests.name: build_loop_steps_complete_in_order
tests.details: All five steps (tests_written, tests_verified_failing, implemented, security_audit, documentation_updated) completed sequentially with stub artifacts.

tests.name: feat_commit_succeeds_in_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded with active build loop.

tests.name: test_gate_record_feature
tests.details: scripts/test-gate.sh --record-feature 'uat-feat-1' reported '[OK] Feature recorded (1/2 until next test session)'.

tests.name: feature_recorded_step_completed
tests.details: build_loop:feature_recorded marked complete (6/6); build_loop reset for next feature.

tests.name: recorded_feature_in_progress
tests.details: build-progress.json features_completed contains 'uat-feat-1'.

documentation_gaps.gap: process-checklist.sh has no --start-phase1 command (only --start-phase3 / --start-phase4); runbook Section B references --start-phase1. Clarify how Phase 1 is started/tracked in the canonical script.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational completed and reported 'Upgrade Complete'. Note: the runbook's literal command --upgrade-deployment private_poc is REJECTED by the framework with '[FAIL] Invalid target deployment: private_poc (must be: personal, organizational)' — see U-J/runbook gap below. To exercise the upgrade I substituted 'organizational' (the documented value).

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: .current_phase preserved (0). .project preserved ('uat-agent-60'). Only addition: 'last_upgrade': '2026-04-25'.

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: diff against pre-upgrade snapshot is empty; build_loop history and phase2_init steps unchanged.

tests.name: build_progress_preserved
tests.details: features_completed still contains 'uat-feat-1'; counters unchanged.

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Output includes 'Next steps for organizational deployment: Fill in Pre-Phase 0 organizational pre-conditions in APPROVAL_LOG.md; Complete Section 8 of PROJECT_INTAKE.md (governance pre-flight); Assign approval authorities for each phase gate.'

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json before upgrade; first invocation refused with '[FAIL] upgrade blocked — pending user decision.' (PR #17 U-F fix verified). After --clear, upgrade proceeded.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header Changelog block mentions BL-006, BL-015, and BL-016.

tests.name: no_template_placeholders_in_output
tests.details: grep of upgrade output for __PROJECT_NAME__/__PLATFORM__/__LANGUAGE__/__TRACK__ returned 0 hits in the user-facing output (the placeholder strings exist only inside scripts/reconfigure-project.sh as sed templates, which is intentional). U-I not reproduced in this run.

tests.name: deployment_field_written_to_phase_state
tests.details: BUG: upgrade reported 'Deployment: organizational' and committed chore(upgrade): deployment personal -> organizational, but .claude/phase-state.json .deployment field STILL reads 'personal' (unchanged from pre-upgrade snapshot). This is U-J confirmation.

tests.name: poc_mode_set_for_private_poc_target
tests.details: Runbook target was private_poc, but framework has no flag on intake-wizard.sh / upgrade-project.sh to set poc_mode during a personal->private_poc upgrade. .poc_mode remains null. There is a --to-sponsored-poc flag but no --to-private-poc. This is a runbook/framework misalignment (separate from U-J).

framework_bugs.summary: U-J confirmation: phase-state.json .deployment field not written during personal->organizational upgrade
framework_bugs.reproduction: Build personal/mcp_server/full project; bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational; cat .claude/phase-state.json — .deployment is still 'personal' despite upgrade output and commit message saying 'personal -> organizational'.
framework_bugs.expected: .claude/phase-state.json .deployment should equal 'organizational' after the upgrade (since the upgrade also rewrites APPROVAL_LOG.md / CLAUDE.md / PROJECT_INTAKE.md to organizational form).
framework_bugs.actual: deployment field unchanged ('personal'). Only 'last_upgrade' key was added. Other fields (current_phase, track, project, poc_mode, compliance_ready) also unchanged. Confirms U-J still open.

framework_bugs.summary: intake-wizard --upgrade-deployment rejects scenario values (private_poc, sponsored_poc); only personal/organizational accepted
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment private_poc -> '[FAIL] Invalid target deployment: private_poc (must be: personal, organizational)'.
framework_bugs.expected: Either the runbook should call upgrade-deployment with personal|organizational and a separate gov-mode/poc-mode flag, OR upgrade-deployment should accept the four scenario names defined in init.sh (personal, private_poc, sponsored_poc, production).
framework_bugs.actual: The deployment value space (personal|organizational) and the scenario value space (personal|private_poc|sponsored_poc|production) are not unified at the upgrade entry point. There is also no explicit --to-private-poc flag (--to-sponsored-poc and --to-production exist). Runbook Section F's literal command does not work for private_poc/sponsored_poc targets without manually specifying organizational and then editing poc_mode.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook Section F says 'bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment {TARGET_DEPLOYMENT}' where TARGET_DEPLOYMENT comes from the private_poc/sponsored_poc scenario name — but intake-wizard accepts only personal|organizational. Either add --to-private-poc, accept the scenario names, or revise the runbook to call --upgrade-deployment organizational and then a separate poc-mode-set step.
documentation_gaps.where: RUNBOOK.md Section F lines 286-294 + scripts/intake-wizard.sh upgrade-deployment branch

documentation_gaps.gap: pending-approval.sh diagnostic message printed '(missing)' for question text and '(unknown)' for offered options when reading a sentinel file with valid JSON. Suggests jq path lookups may not match the documented schema in .claude/pending-approval.json.

Init's verify-install reports manual-action 'Copy from orchestrator: docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md' but the framework's actual filename uses a hyphen (mcp-server.md). Following the suggested copy command would fail.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git ls-files | xargs grep for password=value/api_key=value/secret=value/token=value patterns returned 0 hits. Documentation/CI references to those terms exist but contain no values.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — no FORCE/reset events; .claude/process-audit.log absent as expected.

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find $WORKDIR/.claude -name '*.tmp' returned no results.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches captured starting SHA). git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only '?? .claude/' and '?? Reports/uat-2026-04-26/' (untracked, pre-existing).

summary: Baseline init + Phase 2 + intake-wizard upgrade all completed end-to-end; PR #17 (U-F sentinel respect, U-H helper copying) and PR #18 (framework-self guard) confirmed working; PR #19 (--non-interactive) confirmed working. Two High/Medium framework bugs found: (1) platform module not provisioned for mcp_server (filename mismatch hyphen vs underscore) — High; (2) U-J confirmation — phase-state.json .deployment field not written by upgrade — Medium. Plus a Medium runbook/framework alignment gap on --upgrade-deployment value space (private_poc not accepted).
